tagged_story_for_tts:
"Predictable orbits.<[silence:50ms]> Gravity’s choreography.<[silence:50ms]>
Celestial Mechanics is the study of how objects move in space under gravity’s pull, like planets tracing invisible tracks around the Sun.<[silence:50ms]> Newton’s laws explain it: a planet falls toward the Sun but moves sideways so fast it never hits, like a ball spun on a string.<[silence:50ms]> This dance keeps Earth at 93 million miles from the Sun, year after year.<[silence:50ms]> Three related subjects are orbital resonance, Lagrange points and tidal forces."
clean_story:
"Predictable orbits. Gravity’s choreography.
Celestial Mechanics is the study of how objects move in space under gravity’s pull, like planets tracing invisible tracks around the Sun. Newton’s laws explain it: a planet falls toward the Sun but moves sideways so fast it never hits, like a ball spun on a string. This dance keeps Earth at 93 million miles from the Sun, year after year. Three related subjects are orbital resonance, Lagrange points and tidal forces."
